 {"id":"beads_rust-s9a","title":"Output formats & JSON schema parity (IssueWithCounts/Details/Blocked/TreeNode)","description":"# Output Formats + JSON Schema Parity

## Purpose
Ensure text and JSON outputs match classic bd shapes, including IssueWithCounts/IssueDetails/BlockedIssue/TreeNode and legacy JSON error behavior.

## Core JSON Shapes
- **IssueWithCounts**: Issue fields + `dependency_count`, `dependent_count`.
- **IssueDetails**: Issue fields + `labels`, `dependencies`, `dependents`, `comments`, `parent`.
- **BlockedIssue**: Issue fields + `blocked_by_count`, `blocked_by` (array of IDs).
- **TreeNode**: Issue fields + `depth`, `parent_id`, `truncated`.

## Command JSON Expectations
- `list` / `search`: array of IssueWithCounts.
- `show`: array of IssueDetails (even for single ID).
- `ready`: array of Issue.
- `blocked`: array of BlockedIssue.
- `dep tree`: array of TreeNode.
- `count`: `{count}` or `{total, groups}`.
- `stats/status`: StatusOutput summary.

## Text Output Key Points
- Status icons: ○ open, ◐ in_progress, ● blocked, ❄ deferred, ✓ closed, ✗ tombstone, 📌 pinned.
- List default ordering: priority ASC, created_at DESC.
- Pretty/tree formatting uses dependency tree connectors (`├──`, `└──`).
- Agent mode (if implemented) strips emoji/colors and uses `ID: Title`.

## JSON Error Behavior (legacy quirks)
- Some fatal errors emit `{ "error": "..." }` to **stdout**.
- Others emit JSON error to **stderr**.
- Some commands still print text errors even with `--json`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSON shapes match bd for classic commands.
- Text output matches golden snapshots (see snapshot bead).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:03:24.175642669-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T08:54:07.999179359-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T08:54:07.999179359-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented output formats module with JSON types (IssueWithCounts, IssueDetails, IssueWithDependencyMetadata, BlockedIssue, TreeNode, Statistics) and text formatting functions (format_status_icon, format_priority, format_type_badge, format_issue_line) with status icon constants. All 10 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-s9a","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-0ol","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:45:40.681573467-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-sav","title":"prime Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br prime` command to preload context for AI coding agents. This outputs structured information about ready issues, blocked work, and project state in a format optimized for LLM consumption.

## CLI Interface
```
br prime [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --limit <N>               Max issues to include (default: 10)
  --focus <ID>              Focus on specific issue and its context
  --include-closed          Include recently closed issues
  --json                    Output as JSON (default)
  --markdown                Output as Markdown
```

## Technical Requirements

### Context Assembly
```rust
pub struct AgentContext {
    pub ready_issues: Vec<IssueWithCounts>,
    pub in_progress: Vec<IssueWithCounts>,
    pub recently_closed: Vec<IssueWithCounts>,
    pub blocked_summary: BlockedSummary,
    pub project_stats: ProjectStats,
    pub focus_issue: Option<IssueDetails>,
}

fn assemble_agent_context(opts: &PrimeOptions) -> Result<AgentContext> {
    let storage = open_storage()?;
    
    let ready = storage.get_ready_issues(opts.limit)?;
    let in_progress = storage.list_issues(&ListFilter {
        status: Some(Status::InProgress),
        limit: Some(opts.limit),
        ..Default::default()
    })?;
    
    let recently_closed = if opts.include_closed {
        storage.list_issues(&ListFilter {
            status: Some(Status::Closed),
            since: Some(days_ago(7)),
            limit: Some(5),
            ..Default::default()
        })?
    } else {
        vec![]
    };
    
    let blocked = storage.get_blocked_summary()?;
    let stats = storage.get_project_stats()?;
    
    let focus = opts.focus.as_ref()
        .map(|id| storage.get_issue_details(id))
        .transpose()?;
    
    Ok(AgentContext { ready, in_progress, recently_closed, blocked, stats, focus })
}
```

### Markdown Output Format
```markdown
# Beads Context

## Project Stats
- Total: 156 issues (89 open, 12 in progress, 55 closed)
- Blocked: 41 issues waiting on dependencies
- Velocity: 1.14 issues/day (last 7 days)

## Ready to Work (10 issues)
| ID | Priority | Type | Title |
|----|----------|------|-------|
| bd-abc12 | P0 | bug | Fix authentication timeout |
| bd-def34 | P1 | feature | Add user preferences |
...

## In Progress (2 issues)
| ID | Priority | Title |
|----|----------|-------|
| bd-ghi56 | P1 | Implement caching layer |

## Blocked Summary
- 41 issues blocked
- Top blockers: bd-xyz99 (blocks 5), bd-abc12 (blocks 3)

## Focus: bd-abc12 (if --focus provided)
**Fix authentication timeout**
Type: bug | Priority: P0 | Status: open

### Description
The authentication system times out after 30 seconds...

### Blocked By
- bd-xyz99: Database connection pooling

### Blocking
- bd-def34: Add user preferences (waiting for auth fix)
```

## Use Cases

### Agent Session Start
```bash
# Agent reads context at session start
br prime --limit 5 --json > /tmp/context.json
```

### Focus on Specific Issue
```bash
# Agent working on specific issue
br prime --focus bd-abc12
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Output ready issues (unblocked work)
- [ ] Output in-progress issues (current work)
- [ ] Output project statistics
- [ ] Output blocked summary
- [ ] --focus includes full issue details and dependencies
- [ ] --include-closed shows recent completions
- [ ] JSON output (default)
- [ ] Markdown output
- [ ] Configurable limit

## Unit Tests
- Empty project returns valid (empty) context
- Ready issues sorted by priority
- In-progress issues included
- Focus issue includes dependencies
- Recently closed filtered by date
- Markdown format is valid
- JSON format is valid

## Dependencies
- ready Command (get_ready_issues)
- blocked Command (blocked summary)
- stats Command (project stats)
- show Command (issue details)
- SQLite Storage Layer Core

## Rationale
AI coding agents need structured context to work effectively. The prime command provides this in a consistent format, reducing the need for agents to run multiple commands to understand project state.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:19:29.104863452-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T02:49:17.702231946-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T02:49:17.702231946-05:00","close_reason":"Out of scope for br classic parity (prime command not supported)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-sav","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-4w1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:24:16.281464979-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-sav","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-ja0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:24:16.334886906-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-sav","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-otn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:24:16.388588508-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-tqs","title":"config Command Implementation","description":"# config Command Implementation

## Purpose
Implement `br config` to read/write both YAML-only startup settings and DB-backed runtime settings, mirroring bd behavior and precedence rules.

## CLI
```
br config get <key>
br config set <key> <value>
br config list
br config delete <key>
br config unset <key>   # alias for delete
```

## Behavior
- **YAML-only keys** (see config system bead): read/write `.beads/config.yaml`.
- **DB keys**: stored in SQLite `config` table (direct mode).
- `config list`: lists DB config only, **sorted by key**; warns if YAML/env overrides DB values.
- `config get`: YAML-only keys read from YAML; DB keys read from DB.
- `config set`: writes YAML or DB depending on key.
- `config delete/unset`: removes DB config only (does **not** edit YAML).
- `sync.branch` vs `sync-branch` normalization: YAML uses `sync-branch` key.

## YAML Editing Rules
- Preserve existing file as much as possible (append if missing).
- Booleans lowercased, numbers/durations unquoted; other strings quoted.
- Validate `hierarchy.max-depth >= 1`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Correctly routes keys to YAML vs DB.
- `config list` warns when YAML/env overrides DB values.
- Works with missing DB (no-db mode): YAML-only keys only.

## Tests
- Set/get/delete YAML-only keys.
- Set/get/delete DB keys.
- Precedence warning behavior in list.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:17:16.887872041-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T02:44:57.373558617-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-tqs","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-1md","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:46:24.043290489-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-tqs","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rxg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:52:13.933685794-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-trr","title":"comments Command Implementation","description":"# comments Command Implementation

## Purpose
Implement `br comments` for adding and listing comments, with JSON output parity and proper author resolution.

## CLI
```
br comments <id>                # list
br comments add <id> <text>      # add
br comments add <id> -f <file>   # add from file
```
Alias: `comment` (legacy compatibility).

## Behavior
- Resolve partial IDs before use.
- Author resolution order (if `--author` not provided):
  `--actor` → `BD_ACTOR` → `BEADS_ACTOR` → `git config user.name` → `$USER` → `"unknown"`.
- Add comment:
  - insert row with `created_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`
  - emit `commented` event
  - mark issue dirty
- List:
  - order by `created_at ASC`

## Output
- JSON list: array of Comment objects.
- JSON add: single Comment object.
- Text list: header + `[author] at YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM` with markdown-rendered text.
- Text add: `Comment added to <id>`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSON shapes match bd.
- Event insertion + dirty marking on add.
- No dedupe on add (dedupe only during import).

## Tests
- Add/list round trip.
- Author fallback order.
- JSON vs text output shapes.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:17:23.336348065-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T09:17:35.979301893-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-trr","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-1ce","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:45:58.24764077-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-trr","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-554","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:53:01.578263007-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-trr","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-72y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:53:01.632053821-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-trr","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-h2c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:53:01.684242981-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"beads_rust-u23","title":"reopen Command Implementation","description":"# reopen Command

## Purpose
Explicitly reopen closed issues (distinct from update --status open), matching bd behavior with optional comment insertion.

## CLI
```
br reopen <id...> [--reason <text>]
```

## Flags
- `<id...>`: One or more issue IDs (partial resolution). If none, uses last-touched.
- `--reason <text>`: Reason for reopening, stored as a comment on the issue.

## Behavior
1. Resolve issue ID(s) via partial matching.
2. For each issue:
   - Validate issue is currently closed; warn if already open.
   - Set `status=open`.
   - Clear `closed_at` field (set to null).
   - If `--reason` provided, add a comment: "Reopened: <reason>".
   - Emit `reopened` event to event log.
   - Mark issue as dirty for export.
3. Rebuild blocked cache (reopened issues may become blockers).

## Output

### JSON
Array of reopened Issue objects:
```json
[
  {
    "id": "bd-abc12",
    "title": "Implement feature",
    "status": "open",
    "closed_at": null
  }
]
```

### Text Output
```
✓ Reopened bd-abc12: Implement feature
  Reason: Found additional edge case
```

## Error Handling
- **IssueNotFound**: If ID doesnt resolve → error with suggestions.
- **AmbiguousId**: If ID resolves to multiple → error with candidate list.
- **AlreadyOpen**: Warning (not error) if issue already open.

## Logging
```rust
tracing::info!(id = %issue.id, "Reopening issue");
tracing::debug!(previous_status = ?old_status, "Issue was previously {:?}", old_status);
tracing::info!(id = %issue.id, reason = ?reason, "Issue reopened");
if let Some(reason) = reason {
    tracing::debug!(id = %issue.id, "Adding reopen comment");
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Comment inserted when reason provided.
- closed_at is cleared (set to null).
- JSON shape matches bd.
- Multiple IDs work correctly.

## Unit Tests (storage layer)
```rust
// tests/storage/reopen_tests.rs
test_reopen_closed_issue_basic
test_reopen_sets_status_open
test_reopen_clears_closed_at
test_reopen_marks_dirty
test_reopen_writes_event
test_reopen_with_reason_adds_comment
test_reopen_already_open_returns_warning
test_reopen_nonexistent_issue_fails
test_reopen_updates_blocked_cache
test_reopen_may_block_other_issues
test_reopen_multiple_issues
test_reopen_preserves_other_fields
```

## Integration Tests (CLI)
```rust
// tests/integration/reopen_tests.rs
#[test]
fn test_reopen_basic() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue to reopen");
    
    // Close first
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["close", &id])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Reopen
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["reopen", &id])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("Reopened"));
    
    // Verify status is open
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id, "--json"])
        .assert()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("\"status\":\"open\""));
}

#[test]
fn test_reopen_clears_closed_at() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Check closed_at");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["close", &id])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["reopen", &id])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id, "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    assert!(json[0]["closed_at"].is_null());
}

#[test]
fn test_reopen_with_reason_adds_comment() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "With reason");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["close", &id])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["reopen", &id, "--reason", "Found edge case"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Verify comment was added
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["comments", "list", &id])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("Reopened"))
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("Found edge case"));
}

#[test]
fn test_reopen_already_open_warns() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Already open");
    
    // Issue is already open, reopen should warn
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["reopen", &id])
        .assert()
        .success()
        .stderr(predicate::str::contains("already open").or(predicate::str::is_empty()));
}

#[test]
fn test_reopen_nonexistent_fails() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["reopen", "bd-nonexistent"])
        .assert()
        .failure()
        .stderr(predicate::str::contains("not found"));
}

#[test]
fn test_reopen_multiple_issues() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let id1 = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue 1");
    let id2 = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue 2");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["close", &id1, &id2])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["reopen", &id1, &id2])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Verify both reopened
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["list", "--status", "open", "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(json.as_array().unwrap().len(), 2);
}

#[test]
fn test_reopen_affects_blocked_cache() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let blocker = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Blocker");
    let blocked = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Blocked");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["dep", "add", &blocked, &blocker])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Close blocker, blocked should become ready
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["close", &blocker])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .arg("ready")
        .assert()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("Blocked"));
    
    // Reopen blocker, blocked should become blocked again
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["reopen", &blocker])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .arg("blocked")
        .assert()
        .stdout(predicate::str::contains("Blocked"));
}

#[test]
fn test_reopen_json_output() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "JSON test");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["close", &id])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["reopen", &id, "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    assert!(json.is_array());
    assert_eq!(json[0]["status"], "open");
}

#[test]
fn test_reopen_last_touched() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    let id = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Last touched");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["close", &id])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Reopen without ID uses last-touched
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .arg("reopen")
        .assert()
        .success();
}
```

conformance_test! {
    name: "reopen_basic",
    setup: [
        "create Issue to reopen",
        "close <id1>",
    ],
    br_command: "br reopen <id1> --json",

    setup: [

    ],
    br_command: "br reopen <id1> --reason \"Edge case found\" --json",
    bd_command: "bd reopen <id1> --reason \"Edge case found\" --json",
    compare: ContainsFields(vec!["id", "status"]),
}

 {"id":"beads_rust-u5s","title":"orphans Command (git-commit reference scan)","description":"# orphans Command (git commit reference scan)

## Purpose
Identify open/in-progress issues referenced in git commits but not yet closed. This is a read-only view used to catch missing close operations.

## Behavior (classic)
- Runs `git log --oneline --all` and extracts IDs like `(bd-abc123)`.
- Prefix is read from DB config `issue_prefix` (fallback default `bd`).
- Supports hierarchical IDs (e.g., `bd-abc.1`).
- For each issue ID, keep only the **latest (most recent)** commit reference.
- Only return issues whose status is `open` or `in_progress`.
- If not a git repo, missing `.beads/`, or missing DB: return empty list (no error).

## CLI
```
br orphans [--details] [--fix]
```
- `--details`: include latest commit hash + message in human output.
- `--fix`: **interactive** close flow (no JSON batch). Should prompt for confirmation
  then run `br close <id> --reason Implemented` per issue.

## Output (JSON)
```json
[
  {
    "issue_id": "bd-abc",
    "title": "Fix edge-case",
    "status": "in_progress",
    "latest_commit": "deadbeef",
    "latest_commit_message": "Fix edge-case for parser"
  }
]
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Matches commit parsing and latest-commit selection rules.
- Returns empty list (not error) when git/DB not available.
- JSON shape matches bd.

## Tests
- Fixture repo with commits referencing issue IDs; verify latest commit selection.
- Non-git directory returns empty list with exit 0.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:04:27.819105411-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T02:20:42.94800734-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-u5s","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-gs0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:47:09.358381457-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-uin","title":"prime Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br prime` command for displaying context about the current beads state. This is designed for AI agents to quickly understand the project's issue state after context loss (e.g., after compaction or starting a new session).

## CLI Interface
```
br prime [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --full                      Include all context (ready + in_progress + recent)
  --ready-only                Only show ready issues
  --limit <N>                 Limit issues per category (default: 10)
  --json                      Output as JSON
  --robot                     Machine-readable output
```

## Output Structure

The prime command outputs a comprehensive snapshot for agent context recovery:

```
# Beads Context Recovery
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

## Project: beads_rust
Prefix: beads_rust-
Schema: v3
Issues: 156 total (42 open, 8 in_progress, 106 closed)

## In Progress (8)
Your active work:

[P0] beads_rust-abc123  Fix critical auth bug
     Assignee: alice | Updated: 2 hours ago
     Blocked by: (none - ready to work)

[P1] beads_rust-def456  Implement user dashboard
     Assignee: bob | Updated: 1 day ago
     Blocked by: beads_rust-xyz789 (Config system)

## Ready to Work (top 10)
Unblocked issues by priority:

[P0] beads_rust-ghi789  Security patch for XSS
[P1] beads_rust-jkl012  Add pagination to list API
[P1] beads_rust-mno345  Refactor error handling
...

## Recently Updated (top 5)
Latest activity:

[closed] beads_rust-pqr678  Setup CI/CD pipeline
         Closed 3 hours ago by alice

[updated] beads_rust-stu901  Database migration scripts
          Updated 5 hours ago (status: open → in_progress)

## Critical Blockers
Issues blocking the most work:

1. beads_rust-xyz789 (Config system) - blocks 5 issues
2. beads_rust-vwx234 (Auth middleware) - blocks 3 issues

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Commands: br ready | br show <id> | br update <id> --status=in_progress
```

### Implementation
```rust
fn prime(&self, opts: PrimeOptions) -> Result<PrimeContext> {
    let stats = self.get_stats()?;
    let in_progress = self.list_issues(ListQuery { 
        status: Some(Status::InProgress),
        limit: opts.limit,
        ..Default::default()
    })?;
    let ready = self.get_ready_issues(ReadyFilters {
        limit: opts.limit,
        ..Default::default()
    })?;
    let recent = self.get_recent_activity(opts.limit)?;
    let blockers = self.find_critical_blockers(5)?;
    
    Ok(PrimeContext {
        stats,
        in_progress,
        ready,
        recent,
        blockers,
    })
}
```

## JSON Output
```json
{
  "project": "beads_rust",
  "stats": {
    "total": 156,
    "open": 42,
    "in_progress": 8,
    "closed": 106
  },
  "in_progress": [...],
  "ready": [...],
  "recent": [...],
  "critical_blockers": [...]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Show project summary (name, prefix, counts)
- [ ] List in-progress issues
- [ ] List ready-to-work issues
- [ ] Show recent activity
- [ ] Identify critical blockers
- [ ] Human-readable and JSON output
- [ ] Configurable limits
- [ ] Fast execution (< 100ms)

## Dependencies
- Requires stats Command infrastructure
- Requires ready Command infrastructure
- Requires blocked Command infrastructure

## Rationale
AI agents lose context frequently due to context window limits. The prime command provides everything an agent needs to resume work effectively. It answers: "What was I working on? What can I work on? What's blocking progress?"
